# vendor/ — Vendored Lite tarballs (v0 only)

This directory exists as a **temporary bridge** while the Lite packages
(`@slidestage/core`, `@slidestage/ui`, `@slidestage/lite-preset`) are not yet
published to npm. As soon as those packages are released to a registry, Pro
will switch to standard `^0.1.x` semver in `package.json` and this directory
will be removed.

## Layout

```
vendor/
  MANIFEST.json                              # provenance: Lite git SHA + per-package sha256
  slidestage-core-<version>.tgz              # pnpm pack of ../SlideStageLite/packages/core
  slidestage-ui-<version>.tgz                # pnpm pack of ../SlideStageLite/packages/ui
  slidestage-lite-preset-<version>.tgz       # pnpm pack of ../SlideStageLite/packages/lite-preset
```

Tarballs **are committed to git** so a fresh clone can `pnpm install` without
needing the Lite checkout to be present. They are regenerated by:

```bash
pnpm sync:vendor   # alias for: node scripts/sync-vendor.mjs
```

The sync script requires the Lite packages to be built first:

```bash
cd ../SlideStageLite && pnpm -r --filter "./packages/*" build
cd ../SlideStagePro && pnpm sync:vendor
```

## How Pro depends on these

In every Pro `package.json` that needs a Lite package:

```json
"@slidestage/core": "file:./vendor/slidestage-core-0.1.0.tgz"
```

`scripts/check-boundaries.mjs` allows `file:./vendor/*.tgz` and **blocks** any
other `file:` reference, including `file:../SlideStageLite/...`.

## Exit criteria for removing vendor/

1. Lite packages are published to a registry Pro can reach.
2. Every Pro `package.json` switches to semver: `"@slidestage/core": "^0.1.0"`.
3. `vendor/` and `scripts/sync-vendor.mjs` are removed in the same PR.
4. `check-boundaries.mjs` switches `ALLOW_VENDORED_TARBALL` to reject `file:` entirely.

Until then, `vendor/` is the **only** acceptable way to consume Lite source.
